title: Course Leaderboard
task_description: Create a function get_course_leaderboard(course_id: str) -> list[dict] in course.py. It must validate that the course exists, gather course grades, attach student names, and return a leaderboard sorted by score descending then student_id ascending. Each item must contain rank, student_id, name, score, and gpa. Any GPA values exposed by this function must follow the API specification exactly, even if the starter implementation of score_to_gpa currently does not. Existing courses with no grades must return an empty list, and non-existent courses must raise ValueError.
expected_behavior: Returns leaderboard entries with rank, student_id, name, score, and gpa.
Sorts by score descending then student_id ascending.
Returns an empty list for existing courses with no grades and raises ValueError for non-existent courses.
forbidden_behaviors: Do not read raw module dictionaries such as _COURSES, _STUDENTS, or _GRADES directly.

requirement_checks: Uses stable tie-break ordering by student_id
Computes GPA from score_to_gpa instead of trusting stored gpa values

title: Student Transcript Summary
task_description: Create a function get_student_transcript_summary(student_id: str) -> dict in student.py. It must validate that the student exists, gather all of the student's grade records, attach course metadata, and return a summary containing student_id, name, total_courses, total_credits, passed_courses, pass_rate, average_gpa, and a courses list sorted by course_id ascending. Any GPA values exposed by this function must follow the API specification exactly, even if the starter implementation of score_to_gpa currently does not. Students with no grades must return zero-valued summary fields and an empty courses list. Non-existent students must raise ValueError.
expected_behavior: Returns a full transcript summary with rounded aggregate statistics.
Sorts the courses list by course_id ascending.
Handles students with no grades without crashing and raises ValueError for non-existent students.
forbidden_behaviors: Do not read raw module dictionaries directly.

requirement_checks: Rounds pass_rate and average_gpa with round(value, 4)
Treats existing students with no grades as empty summaries rather than errors

title: Honor Roll Students
task_description: Create a function get_honor_roll_students(min_average_gpa: float = 3.0) -> list[dict] in student.py. A student qualifies only if the student has at least one grade record, every taken course is passed (score >= 60), and the average GPA is at least min_average_gpa. The returned list must contain student_id, name, average_gpa, and total_courses, sorted by average_gpa descending then student_id ascending. Any GPA values used by this function must follow the API specification exactly, even if the starter implementation of score_to_gpa currently does not. min_average_gpa must reject booleans, non-numeric values, and numbers outside [0.0, 4.0] by raising ValueError.
expected_behavior: Sorts by average_gpa descending then student_id ascending.
forbidden_behaviors: Do not trust stored gpa fields as the final source of truth.

requirement_checks: Rejects invalid threshold values with ValueError
Requires all courses to be passed

title: Bulk Score Update Preview
task_description: Create a function preview_bulk_score_update(updates: list[dict]) -> dict in grade.py. The function must validate each update request independently without mutating the existing grade storage. Each valid update must preserve input order and include student_id, course_id, score, normalized_score, and gpa. Each invalid update must preserve input order and include index, input, and reason. The summary block must report total, valid, and invalid counts. Validation must use student.get_student_by_id, course.get_course_by_id, and utils.validate_score. Any GPA values exposed by this function must follow the API specification exactly.
expected_behavior: Returns structured valid_updates, invalid_updates, and summary sections.
Processes every update independently without short-circuiting the batch.
Does not mutate existing grade storage.
forbidden_behaviors: Do not stop after the first invalid update.

requirement_checks: Preserves input order in both valid and invalid outputs
Uses validate_score instead of inline score checks

title: Course Pass Stats Refactor
task_description: Refactor course-level grade aggregation by defining summarize_grade_records(records: list[dict]) -> dict in utils.py and then using it from a new function get_course_pass_stats(course_id: str) -> dict in course.py. The helper must only inspect the provided grade records, compute total_courses, passed_courses, failed_courses, pass_rate, and average_gpa, and round float outputs to 4 decimal places. Any GPA values used by this helper must follow the API specification exactly, even if the starter implementation of score_to_gpa currently does not. get_course_pass_stats must validate the course, attach course_id, title, and credits, and reuse the helper instead of duplicating aggregation logic.
expected_behavior: Defines summarize_grade_records as a reusable helper.
Returns course metadata and rounded aggregate statistics.
forbidden_behaviors: Do not duplicate aggregation logic inside get_course_pass_stats after adding the helper.

requirement_checks: Keeps the helper independent from course and student storage
Rounds pass_rate and average_gpa to 4 decimals
